data.title: SA Analyst Rating Upgrades: AAPL, CMI, CLS, NWG
data.description:
A roundup of the most recent upgrades issued by SA analysts.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL). Sell to buy by Johnny Zhang. "After the selloff, the stock's valuation has become more attractive, with its non-GAAP P/E fwd 10% below the 5-year average and in deep oversold territory."

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL). Hold to buy by Jeffrey Fischer. "While I'm not expecting AAPL stock to rise (on an absolute basis) from its recent value of ~$170, I do believe it is most likely to outperform the S&P 500 (SPY) in the near term."

Cummins (NYSE:CMI). Hold to buy by Sandpiper Investment Research. "At 12.0x P/E and 7.5x EV/EBITDA, Cummins offers a strong entry point, with long-term positives outweighing short-term tariff and recession concerns."

Celestica (NYSE:CLS). Hold to buy by Danil Sereda. "I think tariff fears are overblown; CLS's business model can absorb rising costs, with a potential 37% upside to $96.6/share by year-end."

NatWest (NYSE:NWG). Hold to buy by Labutes IR. "With a forward dividend yield of about 7% and a solid capital position, NWG stock is an attractive income play in the European banking sector."

data.title: Simply good foods targets 12% growth in Quest and OWYN retail sales for FY2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: The Simply Good Foods Company (SMPL) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Geoff Tanner highlighted strong momentum in the quarter, driven by 15% net sales growth, including 4% organic growth. Quest and OWYN, which represent 70% of the company's portfolio, achieved retail sales growth of 12% and 57%, respectively. Atkins experienced a decline as expected.
  • Tanner announced increased retail takeaway growth expectations for Quest, now targeting low double digits for the fiscal year, supported by innovation in salty snacks, national tests at key retailers, and successful advertising campaigns.
  • The company introduced Quest Overload bars and Quest milkshakes, both reflecting efforts to expand their product portfolio and target new market opportunities.
  • CFO Shaun Mara reported adjusted EBITDA growth of 18%, reaching $68 million for the quarter, with net income increasing by 10.9% to $36.7 million. He acknowledged improved financial leverage, with net debt to trailing 12-month adjusted EBITDA reduced significantly.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed fiscal year 2025 guidance, projecting total net sales growth of 8.5%-10.5% and adjusted EBITDA growth of 4%-6%, despite challenges in inflation and tariffs.
  • OWYN is expected to contribute $140-$150 million in net sales for the fiscal year. The company remains confident in its target to double OWYN's net sales within three to four years.
  • Quest's retail takeaway growth for FY2025 is now anticipated in the low double digits, driven by product expansion and increased availability.

Financial Results

  • Total net sales for Q2 2025 were $359.7 million, with 4.4% stemming from organic growth and a significant contribution from OWYN.
  • Gross margin declined by 120 basis points year-over-year to 36.2%, affected by inflation and the inclusion of OWYN.
  • Adjusted diluted EPS was $0.46 compared to $0.40 in the prior-year period, supported by volume growth and cost discipline.
  • The company repaid $50 million in term loan debt during the quarter and aims to finish the fiscal year with net leverage around 0.5 times.

Q&A

  • John Baumgartner of Mizuho Securities inquired about the Atkins sales guidance reduction. CEO Tanner explained that the updated guidance reflects further distribution losses at a key club customer, but noted that these losses will be partially offset by gains in Quest and OWYN.
  • Megan Clapp from Morgan Stanley asked about the gross margin outlook. CFO Mara detailed the pressures from cocoa and whey inflation, as well as tariff impacts, but emphasized productivity initiatives aimed at cost mitigation.
  • Analysts also raised questions about Quest's expansion into new categories such as milkshakes and salty snacks. Tanner described these efforts as pivotal for driving further growth and increasing household penetration.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, with concerns over inflationary pressures and Atkins' performance offset by enthusiasm for Quest and OWYN's growth prospects. Questions were focused on understanding margin pressures and the sustainability of growth initiatives.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during the prepared remarks, emphasizing strong category fundamentals and innovation-driven growth. However, they were more measured during the Q&A, particularly when addressing challenges related to tariffs and inflation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Quest's growth has improved from high single digits in Q1 to low double digits in Q2, reflecting increased confidence in salty snacks and new products.
  • Atkins' challenges have deepened, with consumption guidance now reflecting low double-digit declines compared to high single-digit declines previously.
  • OWYN's contribution to sales remains robust, with management reiterating its long-term growth targets.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted tariffs and inflation as significant headwinds impacting gross margin. Efforts to mitigate these include productivity initiatives and cost-saving measures.
  • The Atkins brand faces challenges related to reduced promotional activity and distribution losses, particularly in the club channel.
  • Analysts noted concerns about the competitive landscape in the nutritional snacking category and the potential for further cost pressures.

Final Takeaway

The Simply Good Foods Company delivered strong growth in Q2 2025, driven by robust performance from Quest and OWYN. While challenges persist for Atkins and macroeconomic pressures weigh on margins, management's focus on innovation, category expansion, and financial discipline positions the company for sustained growth. The reaffirmed full-year guidance and increased targets for Quest underscore confidence in the portfolio's resilience and growth potential.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: South Bow declares force majeure on Keystone pipeline after spill
data.description:
Keystone pipeline owner South Bow (NYSE:SOBO) issued a force majeure notice to companies that ship oil on the conduit after a leak in North Dakota released an estimated 3,500 barrels, Bloomberg reported Wednesday.

Keystone may not be able to meet obligations to send oil down the pipeline as of 5 p.m. local time yesterday, according to a notice from South Bow (NYSE:SOBO) that was seen by Bloomberg.

The Keystone pipeline, which runs from Canada into the U.S. and transported an average 624K bbl/day, or more than 26M gal/day, in 2024, was shut after it leaked oil into a field on Tuesday near a pump station in North Dakota; the pipeline carries ~15% of Canada's crude exports to the U.S.

The shutdown is not yet affecting Canadian heavy oil pricing, according to BMO Capital's head of oil and gas research Randy Ollenberger, as there is ample room in storage tanks in Hardisty, Alberta.

"As long as we don't have Keystone down so long that storage tanks start to fill up to capacity, it may not have much impact on the market," Ollenberger said Tuesday.

data.title: Vanguard sounds alarm on recession risk as tariffs cause a shock to the market
data.description:
Vanguard has joined the chorus of analysts and economists ringing alarm bells on recession following U.S. President Donald Trump's imposition of the steepest American trade barriers in over a century. 

"We are dancing with recession," the exchange-traded fund provider said.

Vanguard noted that the effective U.S. tariff rate may exceed 25%, far higher than initial expectations for the year. Historically, tariffs at such levels have been rare, as global markets were less interconnected over a century ago, and Vanguard cautions that sustained tariffs above 20% could have serious economic ramifications. 

One of the larger concerns the ETF issuer outlined was the fact that it was not clear how long tariffs may remain at their announced levels. 

In its updated forecast, Vanguard now predicts U.S. GDP growth in 2025 will fall below 1%, nearly a full percentage point lower than previous estimates. This slowdown threatens to push the economy into "stall speed," increasing the likelihood of a recession. 

Additionally, Vanguard expects inflation to remain persistent, with core inflation projected to reach around 4% by year-end, and unemployment to rise above 5%—the highest levels in a decade, excluding the COVID-19 crisis. 

The firm also warns that the combination of sluggish growth and rising prices could lead to stagflation, creating a tough environment for both stocks and bonds. 

Market Tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (NYSEARCA:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ). 

Fixed Income Tracking ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (NASDAQ:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), (NYSEARCA:BIL), (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).

data.title: 10 Strong Buy listed stocks that are in the green YTD as Wall Street sinks
data.description:
As Wall Street's major market averages continue to experience declines in the aftermath of the new tariffs imposed by President Trump on April 2, which have impacted nations worldwide, not all companies have seen the same level of suffering, with some names faring better than others in these challenging times.

Outlined below are ten U.S. based stocks with market caps of at least $10B that trade both in positive territory in 2025 and hold Strong Buy ratings according to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Metrics. List is ranked per SA quant grades.

No. 10: Ameren Corporation (NYSE:AEE), quant rating of 4.54. 

No. 9: AngloGold Ashanti plc (NYSE:AU), quant rating of 4.57.

No. 8: W. P. Carey (NYSE:WPC), quant rating of 4.63. 

No. 7: Take-Two Interactive Software (NASDAQ:TTWO), quant rating of 4.63. 

No. 6: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER), quant rating of 4.77.

No. 5: RTX Corporation (NYSE:RTX), quant rating of 4.77. 

No. 4: Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM), quant rating of 4.78.

No. 3: Pilgrim's Pride Corporation (NASDAQ:PPC), quant rating of 4.90. 

No. 2: Exelon Corporation (NASDAQ:EXC), quant rating of 4.95. 

No. 1: Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B), quant rating of 4.95. 

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

Year-to-date performance: AEE +5.7%, AU +43.6%, WPC +3%, TTWO +6.6%, UBER +7.8%, RTX +4.1%, PM +24%, PPC +8.1%, EXC +18.2%, and BRK.B +8.6%.

data.title: Trump is said to prepare executive order to revive U.S. shipbuilding
data.description:
President Trump is preparing to sign an executive order as early as Wednesday intended to revive U.S. shipbuilding and to loosen China’s grip on the global shipping industry, a Reuters reporter said in a social-media post that cited sources familiar with the matter.

Huntington Ingalls Industries' (NYSE:HII) stock was up 2.2% by 12:20 p.m. ET amid a broader advance among other aerospace and defense companies. General Dynamics (NYSE:GD) rose 2.7%.

data.title: RxSight downgraded at UBS after Q1 miss and guidance cut
data.description:
RxSight (NASDAQ:RXST) continued to trade lower on Wednesday after UBS downgraded the maker of intraocular lenses following the company’s recent decision to slash its full-year outlook on the back of lower-than-expected preliminary Q1 revenue.

Shares of the Aliso Viejo, California-based firm sold off last week after the announcement, which prompted Bank of America to immediately downgrade the company to Underperform from Buy.

On Tuesday, UBS analyst Danielle Antalffy lowered her rating to Neutral from Buy after revising her two-year CAGR estimates for the company to 15% from 21% CAGR previously and citing a longer path to profitability.

“While we believe long-term growth momentum may not be a significant concern, the broader market faces persistent headwinds, exacerbated by recent tariff policies that heighten recession fears,” Antalffy wrote as she slashed her price target to $16 from $45 per share.

In terms of EV/revenue, the analyst argued that RXST should trade around 3x its 2026 sales, as the company’s SMID-cap peers with similar growth profiles and longer paths to profitability trade at low single-digit multiples.

data.title: Memory stocks in focus as Google says AI hypercomputer runs on new TPU
data.description:
• Memory stocks were in focus on Wednesday as Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) said its new artificial intelligence hypercomputer will run on its latest memory-heavy tensor processing unit.
  • Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) shares fell 1% in midday trading, while American Depository Receipts of Samsung (OTCPK:SSNLF) and SK Hnix (OTCPK:HXSCF) were little changed.
  • “Our new seventh-generation TPU, Ironwood, is designed specifically for thinking and inferential AI models,” Google said in a blog post, referencing its latest TPU. “Ironwood offers five times more peak compute capacity and six times the high-bandwidth memory (HBM) capacity compared to the prior-generation TPU.”
  • Micron, Samsung and SK Hnix largely control the memory market, according to research firm TechInsights.

data.title: Airbus delivers 71 planes in March, lifting year's total to 136
data.description:
Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) on Wednesday said it had delivered 71 commercial airplanes to customers in March, lifting its year-to-date total to 136.

The European aviation giant's tally comes a day after U.S. rival Boeing (NYSE:BA) said it had delivered 41 planes in March, raising its total to 130 for the year.

data.title: TikTok U.S. deal must comply with Chinese law - Beijing spokesperson
data.description:
Any deal for TikTok U.S. (BDNCE) has to be in compliance with the Chinese law, a spokesperson for China’s commerce ministry said Wednesday.

The statement from China comes following a deadline extension by U.S. President Donald Trump for the short video content app from the sale-or-ban law passed under the Biden administration.

The spokesperson for the country said China "opposes practices that ignore the laws of the market economy, plunder by force, and damage the legitimate rights and interests of enterprises. The specific business arrangements must comply with Chinese law, including the export of technology, which must be approved by the Chinese government."

A deal to spin off TikTok's U.S. operations was put on hold after the Chinese government indicated it would not approve the deal under Trump's reciprocal tariff threat.

Earlier this month, a spokesperson for ByteDance told Seeking Alpha that the company "has been in discussion with the U.S. government regarding a potential solution for TikTok U.S. An agreement has not been executed. There are key matters to be resolved. Any agreement will be subject to approval under Chinese law."
